summary: The authors propose LoPT (Local-Learning Post-Training), a strategy that reduces memory cost and increases efficiency by placing a gradient boundary at the transformer midpoint, using a feature-reconstruction objective for the first half and the task objective for the second half.

blocks.content: # Rethinking Local Learning: A Cheaper and Faster Recipe for LLM Post-Training

source_span.quote: # Rethinking Local Learning: A Cheaper and Faster Recipe for LLM Post-Training

blocks.content: LLM post-training typically propagates task gradients through the full depth of the model.

source_span.quote: LLM post-training typically propagates task gradients through the full depth of the model.

blocks.content: We argue that this full-depth backward coupling can be unnecessarily expensive and intrusive, particularly when post-training supervision is much narrower than pre-training.

source_span.quote: We argue that this full-depth backward coupling can be unnecessarily expensive and intrusive, particularly when post-training supervision is much narrower than pre-training.

blocks.content: To this end, we propose \textbf{LoPT}: Local-Learning Post-Training, a simple post-training strategy that makes gradient reach an explicit design choice.

source_span.quote: To this end, we propose \textbf{LoPT}: Local-Learning Post-Training, a simple post-training strategy that makes gradient reach an explicit design choice.

blocks.content: LoPT places a single gradient boundary at the transformer midpoint: the second-half block learns from the task objective, while the first-half block is updated by a lightweight feature-reconstruction objective to preserve useful representations and maintain interface compatibility.

source_span.quote: LoPT places a single gradient boundary at the transformer midpoint: the second-half block learns from the task objective, while the first-half block is updated by a lightweight feature-reconstruction objective to preserve useful representations and maintain interface compatibility.

blocks.content: Extensive experiments demonstrate that LoPT achieves competitive performance with lower memory cost, higher training efficiency and better retention of pretrained capabilities.

source_span.quote: Extensive experiments demonstrate that LoPT achieves competitive performance with lower memory cost, higher training efficiency and better retention of pretrained capabilities.

blocks.content: LoPT shortens the task-induced backward path while limiting direct interference from narrow task gradients on early-layer representations.

source_span.quote: LoPT shortens the task-induced backward path while limiting direct interference from narrow task gradients on early-layer representations.
